Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID COOK, COOK COLLECTION ATTORNEYS'Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration.

Defendants Cook Collection Attorneys, P.C. and David Cook's motion to compel arbitration and stay this action is granted. The parties are required to arbitrate all of plaintiff Joel Rubin's claims in Washington, D.C. per the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The FAA applies to this case. Mr. Rubin's complaint demonstrates he resides in Maryland and Mr. Cook's declaration demonstrates that the work was supposed to occur in Washington, D.C. California law does not apply to work performed by a nonresident outside the state. State statutes are presumed not to have extraterritorial effect. Mr. Rubin's California law defenses to the enforcement of the arbitration agreement fail. Labor Code 299 does not prevent arbitration because Mr. Rubin fails to demonstrate an employment relationship. The evidence before the court shows that the parties had an independent contractor relationship. Mr. Rubin fails to show that Mr. Cook controlled the manner and means of Mr. Rubins work. The parties' agreement indicates a "contractor" relationship. Mr. Rubin was engaging in specialized work. Mr. Rubin, a sophisticated lobbyist, also fails to show that the agreement was unconscionable. The failure to provide the AAA rules is not problematic since Mr. Rubin could easily have obtained a copy by accessing the internet and his claim of unconscionability is not based on the substance of the AAA rules. (Lane v. Francis Capital Management LLC (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 676, 691-692; Farrar v. Direct Commerce, Inc. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 1257, 1268.) Mr. Rubin's complaint about the costs of the arbitration also fails because this case concerns an arms-length transaction between two sophisticated entities not involving an employment relationship. Mr. Rubin also fails to show that the AAA rules do not require a written award. Order signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, geales@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
